summary: Majority government confirmed, climate grade moved down to D, new public-approval signal at the top, and a readability pass across the dashboard.

items.headline: Climate & Environment moved D+ → D
items.body: Canada is tracking for about half of its required 2030 emissions reduction. Industrial carbon credits are trading near $20/t despite a $95 headline. ECCC is losing 840+ positions. The PM has acknowledged targets will be missed.
drivers: Industrial credits detached from headline price

items.headline: Majority government confirmed
items.body: Carney won a majority government on April 13, 2026 — 174 seats after three byelection wins.

items.headline: Federal gas tax suspension announced
items.body: Federal fuel excise suspended April 14 through September 7. Estimated $2.4B in foregone revenue. La Presse called the move "une décision insensée."

items.headline: Approval Signal added to the top of the dashboard
items.body: 60-day rolling average of approve/disapprove across five CRIC-era pollsters (Léger, Abacus, Ipsos, Angus Reid Institute, Innovative Research). Tracked beside the grades but explicitly not one of them — popularity is not performance.

items.headline: Readability pass on the dashboard
items.body: Plainer card labels, shorter status lines, a new "Limits of this model" note in the Rubric tab, a per-dimension "Last reviewed" line, and an across-the-board font-size bump for mobile.

summary: Climate grade revision (since rolled forward), source stack broadened, probation dimensions normalized to whole-letter C.

items.headline: Source stack expanded
items.body: Added Canadian Climate Institute, IFSD, IRPP / Policy Options, La Presse, Toronto Star, National Observer, and The Conversation Canada to the graded dimensions.

items.headline: Probation dimensions normalized to whole-letter C
items.body: Ethics & Transparency and Flagship Delivery now score as whole letters only (no plus/minus precision behind the displayed grade).
